name: Core Trust Scoring Models MVP
source_doctrine: CyberShield Trust Model Registry and Scoring Requirements
controlling_principle: No score without a model. No model without evidence. No evidence without traceability. No action without consequence.
boundary: These Trust Scoring Models are advisory prototype models. They are not production-calibrated, statistically validated, compliance certifications, legal determinations, live evidence retrieval systems, or live enforcement systems.

models.name: Artifact Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate whether an artifact as a whole is reliable enough for the intended reliance purpose after considering source wrapper, metadata, claims, evidence, and consequence.

factors.label: Source Context
factors.description: Who or what produced the artifact, how it was distributed, and whether the source context is clear.

factors.label: Wrapper Integrity
factors.description: Whether title, date, author, transcript, metadata, and surrounding context are internally consistent.

factors.label: Claim Quality
factors.description: Whether factual claims are specific, separable, and testable.

factors.label: Evidence Alignment
factors.description: Whether claims align with credible supporting evidence.

factors.label: Currency and Scope
factors.description: Whether timing, jurisdiction, domain, and scope match the reliance purpose.

factors.label: Consequence if Wrong
factors.description: Whether the artifact could cause harm, bad decisions, or reputational damage if relied upon incorrectly.

models.name: Claim Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate whether a specific factual claim can be relied on for the current decision purpose.

factors.label: Claim Specificity
factors.description: Whether the claim is precise enough to verify.

factors.label: Primary Source Support
factors.description: Whether a primary or authoritative source supports the claim.

factors.label: Cross-Source Consistency
factors.description: Whether credible sources agree or reveal material conflict.

factors.label: Context Preservation
factors.description: Whether the claim preserves definitions, time period, jurisdiction, and caveats.

factors.label: Evidence Freshness
factors.description: Whether evidence is current enough for the reliance purpose.

factors.label: Risk if Wrong
factors.description: How much damage would result if the claim were false, incomplete, or misapplied.

failure_conditions: no primary source for high-stakes claim

models.name: Sentence-Level Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate a sentence based on whether it contains claims, how many claims it contains, and whether those claims are individually reliable enough for reliance.

factors.label: Claim Density
factors.description: How many factual assertions are packed into the sentence.

factors.label: Claim Separability
factors.description: Whether the sentence can be decomposed into verifiable claim units.

factors.label: Lowest Claim Score
factors.description: The weakest claim in the sentence constrains sentence-level reliance.

factors.label: Evidence Coverage
factors.description: Whether all material claims in the sentence have evidence.

factors.label: Context and Qualification
factors.description: Whether the sentence preserves important caveats and conditions.

factors.label: Reliance Consequence
factors.description: How harmful it would be to quote, brief, or act on the sentence incorrectly.

models.name: Evidence Trust Model
models.purpose: Evaluate whether a piece of evidence is reliable, current, relevant, and strong enough to support the claim or decision at hand.

factors.label: Source Authority
factors.description: Whether the evidence source is authoritative for the specific claim type.

factors.label: Directness
factors.description: Whether the evidence directly supports the claim or only indirectly relates.

factors.label: Freshness
factors.description: Whether the evidence is current enough for the decision purpose.

factors.label: Scope Match
factors.description: Whether the evidence matches the jurisdiction, time period, population, system, or business scope.

factors.label: Integrity
factors.description: Whether the evidence appears complete, unaltered, and traceable.

factors.label: Independence
factors.description: Whether the evidence is independent or self-serving.

models.name: Confidence Model
models.purpose: Estimate how confident CyberShield should be in the score based on evidence quality, completeness, agreement, and uncertainty.

factors.label: Evidence Completeness
factors.description: How much of the needed evidence is present.

factors.label: Evidence Quality
factors.description: How strong and authoritative the evidence is.

factors.label: Source Agreement
factors.description: Whether sources agree or conflict.

factors.label: Model Fit
factors.description: Whether the selected model fits the object being scored.

factors.label: Uncertainty Visibility
factors.description: Whether key uncertainty is surfaced rather than hidden.

factors.label: Human Review Status
factors.description: Whether a qualified human reviewed the result when stakes require it.

models.runtime_mapping: Confidence does not replace trust score; it qualifies how much reliance the score deserves.

models.name: Reliance Risk Model
models.purpose: Evaluate the business, legal, operational, reputational, or security risk of relying on an artifact, claim, evidence item, model output, or action.

factors.label: Decision Criticality
factors.description: How important the decision is.

factors.label: Harm if Wrong
factors.description: Potential damage if the information is false, incomplete, or misapplied.

factors.label: Reversibility
factors.description: Whether the action can be reversed easily.

factors.label: Stakeholder Exposure
factors.description: Whether customers, regulators, employees, partners, or public audiences are affected.

factors.label: Evidence Gap Severity
factors.description: How severe the missing or weak evidence is.

factors.label: Time Pressure
factors.description: Whether urgency increases the chance of poor reliance.

models.runtime_mapping: Higher reliance risk can force Escalate, Block, Refuse, or Quarantine even when trust score is moderate.

models.name: Runtime Admissibility Model
models.purpose: Evaluate whether an action should be allowed, allowed with caveat, constrained, escalated, blocked, refused, or quarantined before runtime execution or leadership reliance.

factors.label: Trust Score
factors.description: Underlying trust score for the object or claim.

factors.label: Confidence Score
factors.description: Confidence in the trust score.

factors.label: Reliance Risk
factors.description: Risk of relying on the information or allowing the action.

factors.label: Required Evidence Present
factors.description: Whether mandatory evidence exists for the action.

factors.label: Owner Authority
factors.description: Whether the accountable owner has authority and is visible.

factors.label: Policy or Purpose Constraint
factors.description: Whether the action violates stated constraints or refusal rules.

models.runtime_mapping: Produces the runtime action ladder output for the decision record.

mvp_rule: V56.4 creates the core model definitions and UI inspection surface only. It does not perform live claim extraction, live scoring, live evidence retrieval, or Internet Trust Engine implementation.
